blockers.description: GLM-5.1 model via NVIDIA NIM produces empty tool call results for memex_recall/memex_retro — tools return {} Tool not found
blockers.workaround: Use bash/read/write tools directly instead. The memex CLI works fine (v0.3.2), the issue is in the GLM model's tool call formatting being mangled by the NIM chat template

human_actions_pending.action: Test the integration in a live Home Assistant instance
human_actions_pending.context: All code changes are pushed to GitHub but haven't been tested in a running HA instance

human_actions_pending.action: Consider switching to a different model if tool call issues persist
human_actions_pending.context: GLM-5.1 on NIM has known chat template bugs with tool results; extension has workarounds but some tools still fail

decisions.decision: Use vol.All(vol.Coerce(float), vol.Range(min=0)) instead of custom validator
decisions.rationale: voluptuous_serialize can't convert custom Python functions; this gives both validation AND proper UI valueMin hints
HA only accepts ISO 4217 codes for MONETARY sensors; CAD/kWh is composite. Unit ¢/kWh without device_class is cleaner
Confirmed by IESO XSD schema (FirstEstimateRate in $/MWh), live data (99.1), and conversion math ($/MWh → ¢/kWh = divide by 10)

decisions.decision: Service registration in async_setup not async_setup_entry
decisions.rationale: Per HA developer docs best practice; services should exist before any config entry is configured
HA's retry logic works when the exception propagates; wrapping in try/except prevented retries
Required for async_config_entry_first_refresh to work properly and for HA to track config entry state

decisions.decision: Raise UpdateFailed from _async_update_data instead of raw exceptions
decisions.rationale: HA handles UpdateFailed gracefully (marks coordinator as failed, retries); raw exceptions are less well-handled

decisions.decision: Read admin_fee from entry.options with fallback to entry.data
decisions.rationale: Options flow saves to entry.options; reconfigure saves to entry.data. Both paths must work

next_action: Design and implement predictive sensors: fetch IESO predispatch/predisp price data for 24h forecast, create 'best_time_to_run' sensor that identifies cheapest window for power-hungry appliances (pool pump, AC)
context_notes: User asked about adding predictive sensors to help decide when to run power-hungry appliances (pool pump, AC). Need to research IESO predispatch data availability, design sensor architecture (forecast coordinator, best-time sensor), and implement. All existing bug fixes (21 issues found and fixed) are pushed to GitHub. Negative LMP values confirmed flowing correctly through pipeline. The GLM-5.1 NIM model has tool call issues with memex tools specifically — other tools (bash, read, write, edit) work fine.
